commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an OpenAI-compatible generic embedding provider to the existing OpenAI plugin, bridges explicit memory embedding configs to generic embedding providers, updates SDK option types/docs/changelog, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: this is a feature PR rather than a current-main bug, but the blocking finding is clear from the PR head because string apiKey values return before SecretRef coercion while the PR docs use ${ZHIPU_API_KEY}. No runtime test was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: There is a narrow, mechanical repair target on the PR branch: preserve existing SecretRef resolution semantics for the new openai-compatible provider before maintainer API review continues.

target.title: feat(openai): add generic OpenAI-compatible embeddings
target.body:
## Summary

This replaces Mariano Belinky's stacked embedding follow-ups with one PR to `main` that keeps the OpenAI-compatible provider inside the existing OpenAI plugin.

- Brings in Mariano's memory-core bridge from #84991 so explicit memory embedding configs can consume generic `embeddingProviders`.
- Adds Mariano's OpenAI-compatible generic embedding provider behavior from #84998, but registers it from `extensions/openai` instead of adding a new `extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings` package.
- Declares `contracts.embeddingProviders: ["openai-compatible"]` on the existing OpenAI plugin and registers `api.registerEmbeddingProvider(...)` from the OpenAI plugin entry.
- Keeps memory-specific providers winning first and keeps generic providers out of memory auto-selection.
- Preserves env SecretRef resolution for `agents.*.memorySearch.remote.apiKey` and configured provider aliases such as `models.providers.<id>.api = "openai-compatible"`.
- Defers Mariano's deprecation-only #85072 so `memoryEmbeddingProviders` remains compatibility without adding early deprecation warnings in this PR.
- Updates the scratch implementation plan to record this replacement landing path.

Credit: implementation is based on Mariano Belinky's work in #84991 and #84998, with commits preserved where practical and adapted for the existing `extensions/openai` placement requested by maintainers.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/embedding-provider.contract.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts` -> passed, 6 files / 32 tests before the autoreview follow-up.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts` -> passed, 5 files / 31 tests after the autoreview follow-up.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` -> passed, 3 files / 28 tests after formatting.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-openai-generic-embedding-provider.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-openai-generic-embedding-provider.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/embedding-providers.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts extensions/openai/index.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check` -> passed.
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check` -> passed.
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs` -> passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/adding-capabilities.md docs/plugins/manifest.md docs/plugins/memory-lancedb.md docs/plugins/plugin-inventory.md docs/plugins/reference.md docs/plugins/reference/openai.md docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `pnpm docs:list` -> passed.
- Raw local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint proof against already-installed `qwen2.5:3b` -> returned one 2048-dimensional embedding.
- New provider proof against local Ollama `qwen2.5:3b` -> query and batch embeddings returned 2048-dimensional vectors.
- Memory-core bridge proof against local Ollama `qwen2.5:3b` through the existing OpenAI plugin -> query and batch embeddings returned 2048-dimensional vectors.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory-core can consume a generic OpenAI-compatible embedding provider through the existing OpenAI plugin, without adding a separate OpenAI-compatible embeddings extension and without making generic providers memory auto-selection candidates.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on `feat/openai-generic-embedding-provider`, with local Ollama 0.24.0 and already-installed model `qwen2.5:3b`. No model was downloaded for this proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused Vitest commands listed above; called raw local Ollama `POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1/embeddings` with model `qwen2.5:3b`; ran a `node --import tsx` script that used `openAICompatibleEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create(...)` against the same Ollama endpoint; ran a second `node --import tsx` script that registered the existing OpenAI plugin with `ownerPluginId: "openai"`, resolved `provider: "openai-compatible"` through memory-core `createEmbeddingProvider(...)`, and called `embedQuery` plus `embedBatch`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "rawOllama": {
    "ok": true,
    "count": 1,
    "dimensions": 2048,
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "error": null
  },
  "providerProof": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "queryDimensions": 2048,
    "batchCount": 2,
    "batchDimensions": [2048, 2048]
  },
  "memoryBridgeProof": {
    "registered": [{ "ownerPluginId": "openai", "id": "openai-compatible" }],
    "requestedProvider": "openai-compatible",
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
    "queryDimensions": 2048,
    "batchCount": 2,
    "batchDimensions": [2048, 2048]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: explicit memory config for `provider: "openai-compatible"` resolves through the generic provider registry owned by the existing OpenAI plugin, posts OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` requests to local Ollama, preserves memory runtime metadata, returns real 2048-dimensional vectors, and leaves memory `auto` selection unchanged.

What was not tested: external hosted OpenAI-compatible services beyond local Ollama were not called.
target.author: dutifulbob
